scope_note: QA Fibonacci Wall-Sun-Sun Lifting to p^2 cert [429]. Primary sources: Wall, D.D. (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' American Mathematical Monthly 67(6) pp. 525-532 doi:10.2307/2309169 (v_p(F_{k*alpha})=v_p(F_alpha)+v_p(k) for odd prime p | F_alpha; alpha(p)|alpha(p^n); T(p^n)=p^{n-1}*T(p) for non-WSS p; foundational for entire [416]-[429] chain); Sun, Z.H. and Sun, Z.W. (1992) 'Fibonacci and Lucas numbers and powers of five' Acta Arithmetica 60 pp. 371-388 doi:10.4064/aa-60-4-371-388 (WSS property: p^2 | F_{p-(5/p)}; connection to Fermat's Last Theorem first case; conjectured no WSS prime exists; known bound > 2*10^11 at publication); Lengyel, T. (1995) 'The order of the Fibonacci and Lucas numbers' Fibonacci Quarterly 33(3) pp. 234-239 (LTE for Lucas sequences: v_p(F_{p*n})=v_p(F_n)+1 for odd prime p with p | F_n; explicit formula for all n). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): (C1) Non-WSS criterion: v_p(F_{alpha(p)})=1 for all primes p in [5,500]; i.e., F_{alpha(p)} = 0 mod p (from [428] C3) but != 0 mod p^2; 93/93 primes PASS; a counterexample would be the first known Wall-Sun-Sun prime (current lower bound > 9.7*10^14 from computational search). (C2) LTE lifting: F_{p*alpha(p)} = 0 mod p^2 for all primes in [5,500]; proof: LTE for Lucas sequences gives v_p(F_{p*alpha})=v_p(F_alpha)+v_p(p)=1+1=2; 93/93 PASS. (C3) alpha lifting: alpha(p^2)=p*alpha(p) for all primes in [5,500]; proof: alpha(p)|alpha(p^2)|p*alpha(p) (Wall range theorem); C2 confirms p*alpha is a zero mod p^2; C1 confirms alpha is not a zero mod p^2; p prime -> ratio in {1,p}; C1 excludes 1; 93/93 PASS verified directly by checking both zeros. (C4) Pisano lifting: T(p^2)=p*T(p) for all primes in [5,300]; two sub-checks: (a) (F_{p*T},F_{p*T+1})=(0,1) mod p^2 [verified by fast doubling] shows T(p^2)|p*T(p); (b) (F_T,F_{T+1})!=(0,1) mod p^2 shows T(p^2)>T(p); with Wall structure T(p^2)/T(p) in {1,p} and (b) excluding 1: T(p^2)=p*T(p); 60/60 primes PASS. CONNECTS TO CHAIN: [416](alpha exists) + [428](alpha|p-(5/p)) + [429](alpha(p^2)=p*alpha(p)) -> p-adic tower alpha(p^n)=p^{n-1}*alpha(p). THEOREM NT: all pure integer; fast doubling mod p^2 stays integer (no float division); no observer float. 4 checks PASS; self-test ok. Derived 2026-06-15.
